touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn InMail sent to Aiden Cooper introducing our CRM automation platform and requesting a brief conversation about their current workflow challenges.
Cold email sent to Aiden Cooper highlighting automation ROI for managed IT firms and offering a 15-minute discovery call.

Connected with Aiden Cooper for a brief 7-minute call. He expressed interest in automating their CRM workflows as their CS team is spending too much time on manual data entry. Agreed to a longer qualification call next week.

questions_asked.question: What are your biggest pain points with your current CRM process?
questions_asked.answer: Our CS team spends about 30% of their time on manual data entry and follow-ups. It's eating into customer face time.

next_steps: Schedule qualification call for next Tuesday

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call attempt to Aiden Cooper went to voicemail. Left message referencing our previous conversation and confirming Tuesday's qualification call.
Aiden replied to calendar invite confirming Tuesday's qualification call and asking for a brief agenda in advance. Sent overview of topics to cover.

questions_asked.question: Can you send an agenda for Tuesday's call so I can prepare?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely. I'll cover current workflow, team size, integration needs, and success metrics. Should take about 20 minutes.

next_steps: Prepare qualification call materials

touches.ai_summary: 18-minute qualification call with Aiden. Discussed current CRM setup, team of 47 CS/sales users, and integration requirements with their IT systems. Aiden mentioned they're evaluating Fathom as well. Strong use case fit for unlimited automation tier.

questions_asked.question: How many users would need access to the automation platform?
questions_asked.answer: We'd need to cover our entire revenue team—about 47 people across CS, sales, and RevOps.

questions_asked.question: What integrations do you support with existing IT infrastructure?
questions_asked.answer: We integrate with all major CRMs, communication tools, and can work with your IT team on custom integrations.

next_steps: Schedule discovery call with broader team
Send integration documentation

touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message sent to Aiden with case study from similar managed IT firm that achieved 40% reduction in admin time.
Aiden confirmed discovery call for Friday and asked to include Oliver Nguyen (IT Director) to discuss technical requirements and security protocols.

questions_asked.question: Can we include our IT Director to discuss security and data handling?
questions_asked.answer: Definitely. We'll cover SOC 2 compliance, data encryption, and integration architecture.

next_steps: Add Oliver Nguyen to discovery call

touches.ai_summary: 20-minute discovery call with Aiden and Oliver. Walked through current pain points in their CRM workflow. Oliver raised questions about data residency and API security. Aiden shared that Fathom is also in consideration but appreciates our automation depth.

questions_asked.question: Where is customer data stored and how do you handle data residency requirements?
questions_asked.answer: We offer US, EU, and APAC data centers. You can specify residency requirements at implementation.

questions_asked.question: What's your current evaluation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: We're hoping to make a decision by end of next month. We're looking at Fathom and your solution.

next_steps: Schedule technical deep-dive
Send security documentation to Oliver

touches.ai_summary: 24-minute discovery call focused on specific automation use cases. Aiden walked through their customer onboarding workflow and renewal tracking process. Identified opportunity to save 15+ hours per week per CS rep with automated workflows.

questions_asked.question: What does your current onboarding workflow look like from first touch to activation?
questions_asked.answer: It's highly manual—about 23 touchpoints across email, calls, and documentation. Takes about 6 hours of rep time per new customer.

questions_asked.question: Can your platform handle conditional workflows based on customer segment?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we support complex conditional logic, customer segmentation, and dynamic content personalization.

next_steps: Map out onboarding automation workflow
Schedule follow-up to review workflow design

touches.ai_summary: 7-minute check-in call with Aiden. He confirmed internal stakeholder alignment and wants to bring in Morgan Suzuki (CEO) for exec-level alignment before moving to demo stage.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in the evaluation process?
questions_asked.answer: I'd like to get Morgan, our CEO, involved. This is a strategic initiative and she'll want visibility before we commit to a demo.

next_steps: Schedule call with Morgan Suzuki and Aiden

touches.ai_summary: Brief 2-minute call with Aiden to coordinate Morgan's availability. Settled on Thursday afternoon for executive alignment call.
next_steps: Send calendar invite to Morgan and Aiden for Thursday

touches.ai_summary: 40-minute discovery call with Aiden, Morgan Suzuki (CEO), and Oliver. Morgan focused on ROI, strategic value, and competitive differentiation. Discussed implementation timeline and change management. Morgan expressed that efficiency gains align with their 2026 growth strategy. Oliver confirmed technical feasibility.

questions_asked.question: What kind of ROI have similar customers seen in the first year?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers see 25-40% reduction in admin time within 90 days, translating to $200-400K in productivity gains for your team size.

questions_asked.question: How does your solution compare to Fathom in terms of automation depth?
questions_asked.answer: Fathom focuses on meeting intelligence. We offer end-to-end CRM workflow automation including meetings, emails, deal updates, and custom triggers.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like for you in the first 6 months?
questions_asked.answer: Reducing CS admin time by at least 30% and improving data accuracy in our CRM. We need clean data for forecasting.

objections: We need to be confident this won't disrupt our current operations during implementation
next_steps: Schedule technical demo
Prepare ROI analysis
Share implementation timeline and change management plan

touches.ai_summary: Attempted follow-up call to Aiden went to voicemail. Left message confirming demo scheduled for next week.

Email from Aiden requesting that Ade Nkrumah (Implementation Lead) and Mason Suzuki (RevOps Analyst) join the demo to evaluate technical fit and data requirements. Confirmed attendance for all stakeholders.

questions_asked.question: Can we bring our Implementation Lead and RevOps Analyst into the demo to assess technical fit?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely. We'll tailor the demo to cover implementation, data migration, and RevOps reporting needs.

next_steps: Update demo attendee list
Prepare technical demo customized for PioneerIO

touches.ai_summary: 60-minute technical evaluation with Aiden, Oliver, Ade, and Mason. Deep-dive into API architecture, webhook capabilities, data migration process, and reporting customization. Ade asked detailed questions about rollout strategy. Mason focused on data integrity and reporting flexibility. Strong technical engagement.

questions_asked.question: What's your recommended rollout approach for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: We typically recommend a phased rollout—pilot with 10 users for 2 weeks, then expand in cohorts of 15-20. Full rollout in 6-8 weeks.

questions_asked.question: How do you ensure data integrity during migration from our current system?
questions_asked.answer: We run parallel systems during migration, with automated validation checks and rollback capabilities. 99.9% data accuracy guarantee.

questions_asked.question: Can we build custom dashboards for different stakeholder groups?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, our platform supports role-based custom dashboards with real-time data and exportable reports.

next_steps: Provide technical architecture documentation
Schedule hands-on product evaluation

touches.ai_summary: 53-minute hands-on technical evaluation. Ade and Mason tested the platform with sample PioneerIO workflows. Mason raised concerns about reporting granularity compared to their current custom-built solution. Ade confirmed implementation approach is sound but asked about support during rollout.

questions_asked.question: What level of support do you provide during implementation and the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: You'll have a dedicated implementation manager, weekly check-ins, 24/7 technical support, and on-demand training sessions.

questions_asked.question: How does the reporting compare to your current setup?
questions_asked.answer: It's more limited than our custom solution in some areas, but the out-of-box dashboards cover 90% of what we need.

objections: Reporting granularity doesn't fully match our current custom-built analytics
next_steps: Explore custom reporting options
Schedule demo focused on reporting capabilities

touches.ai_summary: Email from Aiden confirming they want to move forward with a demo but need to see more on reporting customization before proceeding to proposal. Requested focused session on analytics and dashboard configuration.
next_steps: Schedule reporting-focused demo

touches.ai_summary: 36-minute technical session focused on advanced reporting and analytics. Demonstrated custom report builder, API-based data exports, and integration with their BI tools. Mason confirmed this addresses his reporting concerns. Team consensus to move forward with formal demo.

questions_asked.question: Can we export raw data to our BI platform for custom analysis?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we offer real-time API access, scheduled data exports, and direct integrations with Tableau, Power BI, and Looker.

next_steps: Schedule comprehensive demo for full team

touches.ai_summary: 63-minute comprehensive demo with Aiden, Morgan, Oliver, Ade, and Mason. Walked through end-to-end automation workflows tailored to PioneerIO's use cases. Morgan asked about contract terms and pricing structure. Team expressed strong interest. Morgan requested formal proposal with pricing for 47 seats on unlimited automation tier.

questions_asked.question: What does your pricing structure look like for our team size?
questions_asked.answer: Pricing is based on seat count and tier. For 47 seats on unlimited automation, we're looking at approximately $75K annually.

questions_asked.question: What are typical contract terms—annual, multi-year?
questions_asked.answer: We offer 1-year and 3-year agreements. Multi-year contracts include price protection and priority feature requests.

questions_asked.question: What's your target timeline for implementation if we move forward?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally we'd start implementation in early May to be fully live by mid-June before our Q3 planning cycle.

next_steps: Prepare formal proposal with pricing
Include implementation timeline and ROI analysis

touches.ai_summary: 36-minute proposal review call with Aiden and Morgan. Presented formal proposal at $74,500 ACV for 47 seats on unlimited automation tier. Morgan asked about payment terms and discount options for multi-year commitment. Discussed success metrics and implementation support.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on pricing for a multi-year commitment?
questions_asked.answer: For a 3-year agreement, we can offer a 12% discount and lock in pricing—bringing year 1 to approximately $65,500.

questions_asked.question: What are your payment terms?
questions_asked.answer: Standard terms are annual upfront or quarterly. We can discuss monthly billing for multi-year contracts.

next_steps: Send updated proposal with multi-year pricing options
Schedule follow-up to discuss contract terms

touches.ai_summary: 22-minute follow-up call with Aiden. He shared that they're leaning toward annual contract to start, but want to revisit pricing. Mentioned that Fathom came in at a lower price point but doesn't offer the same automation breadth. Asked about discount options for annual agreement.

questions_asked.question: Can you offer any discount on the annual agreement to match our budget?
questions_asked.answer: I can work with my team on a discount for annual. What budget range are you working within?

questions_asked.question: How does Fathom's pricing compare?
questions_asked.answer: They're about 15% lower, but they're focused on meeting notes. We'd need another tool for workflow automation, which adds cost and complexity.

objections: Pricing is slightly above budget, especially compared to Fathom's quote
next_steps: Review discount approval internally
Schedule pricing discussion

touches.ai_summary: 17-minute pricing call with Aiden. Offered 8% discount on annual agreement, bringing total to $68,500. Aiden appreciated the flexibility but wants to confirm with Morgan before committing. Reiterated value differentiation versus Fathom.

questions_asked.question: Does $68,500 work within your budget?
questions_asked.answer: It's closer. I need to check with Morgan, but I think we can make it work. The value is clear compared to Fathom.

next_steps: Follow up with Morgan and Aiden early next week

touches.ai_summary: Email from Aiden confirming Morgan approved moving forward at $68,500 for annual agreement. Requested contract draft and asked about quarterly payment option to ease cash flow.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can structure quarterly payments at $68,500 annual total. I'll include that in the contract.

next_steps: Send contract draft with quarterly payment terms

touches.ai_summary: 22-minute pricing finalization call with Aiden and Morgan. Reviewed contract terms at $68,500 annual with quarterly payments. Morgan requested one adjustment: extending implementation support from 90 days to 120 days given team size. Agreed to include extended support at no additional cost.

questions_asked.question: Can we extend implementation support to 120 days given our team size and rollout complexity?
questions_asked.answer: We can extend to 120 days at no additional cost. I'll update the contract to reflect that.

next_steps: Update contract with 120-day implementation support
Send revised contract for review

touches.ai_summary: Scheduled negotiation call was rescheduled by Aiden due to internal budget meeting conflict. Moved to Friday afternoon. Brief call to confirm new time.
next_steps: Confirm Friday negotiation call

touches.ai_summary: 13-minute pricing call with Aiden. He shared that finance pushed back on quarterly payments and asked if we can match the original $74,500 price with annual upfront payment to simplify procurement. Agreed to revert to $74,500 with annual upfront payment.

questions_asked.question: If we do annual upfront, can we lock in the $74,500 price instead of the discounted rate?
questions_asked.answer: Actually, the $68,500 was already a discount from $74,500. For annual upfront at $74,500, I can add an additional implementation training session.

next_steps: Finalize contract at $74,500 annual upfront with bonus training session

touches.ai_summary: 31-minute negotiation call with Aiden, Morgan, and Oliver. Final contract review at $74,500 annual upfront for 47 seats, unlimited automation tier, 120-day implementation support, and additional training session. Oliver requested security addendum for data handling. Morgan confirmed internal approval and target signature date of April 18th.

questions_asked.question: Can we add a security addendum covering data residency, encryption standards, and breach notification?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely. We have a standard security addendum we can attach. I'll send it over with the final contract.

questions_asked.question: What's your internal timeline for contract signature?
questions_asked.answer: We're targeting signature by April 18th. Legal review should be complete by end of week.

next_steps: Send final contract with security addendum
Coordinate with legal teams for review

touches.ai_summary: Email from Aiden confirming legal review is underway and they're on track for April 18th signature. Requested implementation kickoff meeting be scheduled for April 21st to hit their May start date. Confirmed all stakeholders aligned.
